articles.title: Prehistoric Transition: A newly revealed cave in Israel may reveal secrets of early Neanderthals
articles.description: Rediscovered right on the edge of a bustling town, frantic archeologist calls not only delayed a highway to be through it, but got the route rerouted to a bridge over it. Why is this cave so valuable?
articles.author: Ruth Schuster

articles.title: Israeli universities rise in global rankings, Palestinian institution makes top list
articles.description: The annual QS rankings place six Israeli universities in the global top 1,000, with gains for Tel Aviv University, the Hebrew University and the Technion. Jerusalem's Al-Quds University emerges as the highest-ranked Palestinian institution
articles.author: Ben Kroll

articles.title: Claudia Roden marks 30 years of the book that reshaped Jewish cuisine
articles.description: First published in 1996, Claudia Roden's The Book of Jewish Food remains one of the world's most influential Jewish cookbooks, 30 years on. 'Without question, this is the most meaningful book I have written,' she tells Haaretz. Plus: Three recipes by Roden
articles.author: Israel Sher

articles.title: Majority of Israel's first-time voters believe their ballot can change the country
articles.description: A survey of nearly 600 Israelis aged 18–22 conducted by the Israel Democracy Institute found that 91 percent plan to vote in the upcoming election. While national security topped the agenda for both Jewish and Arab respondents, concerns over the cost of living emerged as the next leading issue. Jewish-Arab relations were cited much lower
articles.author: Linda Dayan

articles.title: Greek Holocaust victims’ belongings from Nazi camp returned to their descendants
articles.description: Items from Neuengamme restituted with help of Greek students who helped track down families of Jews who were deported to the concentration camp The post Greek Holocaust victims’ belongings from Nazi camp returned to their descendants appeared first on The Times of Israel .

articles.title: ‘Zero hour is coming’: How Israel went from negotiating over hostages to the daring raid at Entebbe
articles.description: The rescue operation at Entebbe, whose 50th anniversary is approaching, is the stuff of Israeli legend. A new trove of archival documents shows how the government took the fateful decision — despite the risks The post ‘Zero hour is coming’: How Israel went from negotiating over hostages to the daring raid at Entebbe appeared first on The Times of Israel .

articles.title: Etgar Keret: Celebrated author feels the world is losing its story
articles.description: Leading voice in Israeli literature discusses the art of writing since October 7, dealing with social media via Substack and his new role directing an MFA program in New York The post Etgar Keret: Celebrated author feels the world is losing its story appeared first on The Times of Israel .

articles.title: US feds open probe into alleged antisemitism at leading teacher union
articles.description: Equal Employment Opportunity Commission probing alleged discrimination at the National Education Association, according to Jewish legal group, the Brandeis Center The post US feds open probe into alleged antisemitism at leading teacher union appeared first on The Times of Israel .

articles.title: State exploited October 7 to silence Arab citizens systematically, report says
articles.description: Zulat Institute argues police used the war to suppress free expression unlawfully via false arrests, protest bans, and the persecution of academics The post State exploited October 7 to silence Arab citizens systematically, report says appeared first on The Times of Israel .

articles.title: Thriving Survivors - Sponsored Content
articles.description: In 2005, Bill shot "The Indestructible Spirit" for Chapman University, capturing 100 thriving Holocaust survivors in color. NYU's Hasia Diner joins to discuss post-Holocaust memory. The post Thriving Survivors appeared first on The Times of Israel .

articles.title: Telling The Jewish Story with Jennifer Tepper and Etai Benson - Sponsored Content
articles.description: Noam joins with theater historian Jennifer Tepper and Broadway actor Etai Benson to explore Jewish storytelling, theater, identity, assimilation and the power of authentic narratives. The post Telling The Jewish Story with Jennifer Tepper and Etai Benson appeared first on The Times of Israel .

articles.title: Strong beliefs and open minds: a graduation challenge - Sponsored Content

articles.title: The image of God in the age of AI (w/Tomer Perisco) - Sponsored Content
articles.description: The religious belief that humans are made in God’s image offers ancient wisdom to regulate AI, reminding us to preserve humanity’s unique, sacred status at all costs. The post The image of God in the age of AI (w/Tomer Perisco) appeared first on The Times of Israel .

articles.title: Over 90% of first-time voters plan to cast ballots in upcoming election, poll finds
articles.description: Enthusiasm appears to stem from young adults' belief in the impact voting can have on Israel's future, despite respondents indicating concern about the current state of democracy The post Over 90% of first-time voters plan to cast ballots in upcoming election, poll finds appeared first on The Times of Israel .

articles.title: Israeli study unlocks 4,000 years of climate change and human adaptation
articles.description: University of Haifa co-led study of Carmel Coast mud cores finds ancient Levantine communities adapted to drought rather than simply collapsing or moving away The post Israeli study unlocks 4,000 years of climate change and human adaptation appeared first on The Times of Israel .

articles.title: House education panel advances trio of bills on Jew-hatred, campus BDS
articles.description: “When someone uses the N-word on campus, no one thinks about free speech. No one talks about, ‘Let’s understand what they’re thinking. Let’s have a discussion,’” Rep. Randy Fine said. “But somehow when it came to Jews, everyone wanted to rediscover the idea of free speech.”
articles.author: Andrew Bernard

articles.title: ‘The dawn of an era’

articles.title: American Jewish University Names Rabbi John Carrier senior rabbi and director of Jewish education
articles.description: Carrier joins the Maas Center for Jewish Journeys, bringing more than a decade of experience in teaching, community building and helping people deepen their connection to Judaism.

articles.title: What George Washington really said to the Jews of Newport
articles.description: America’s first president asserted that a minority’s rights are not granted by the majority, but by the covenant of the republic.

articles.title: The Jewish community is making the wrong argument

articles.title: Gantz calls for national unity government to confront Israel’s challenges

articles.title: France inters Jewish historian executed by the Gestapo in Panthéon

articles.title: The war that follows Israel’s soldiers home
articles.description: Mental-health professionals and military officials have warned of a significant rise in psychological distress, highlighting the urgent need for expanded treatment, rehabilitation and long-term support.

articles.title: House ed panel to consider bills targeting Jew-hatred in schools, universities
articles.description: The Education and Workforce Committee will mark up 11 bills, including measures that would require institutions receiving federal funds to strengthen responses to antisemitism complaints.

articles.title: Majority of Americans favor some prayer in public schools, according to Pew study
articles.description: According to the Pew Research Center, 64% of religiously unaffiliated people who participated in a recent study favored student-led group prayer in public schools.
